sprint: Sprint 29 -- V0.27.3 Bug Fixes (drive_summary 12-field + DriveDetector + calibration + drive_counter)
backlogItem: Bug-fix sprint #2 in the V0.27 chain. Per CIO 2026-05-10 chain-end-merge rule: V0.27.3 branches FROM sprint/sprint28-bugfixes-V0.27.2 (cumulative chain), NOT from main. Main waits for the WHOLE V0.27 chain to be 'fully functional working' before any merge. V0.27.3 closes 5 bugs surfaced during V0.27.2 deploy + Spool's 2026-05-10 evening test cycle.

testBaseline.note: Post-V0.27.2 baseline (V0.27.2 sprint-tip @ 61ec3c7). Sprint 28 added 4149 fast tests per Rex Session 188 close. Verify exact count at sprint start.

sprintNotes: GOAL: Close the 4 bugs queued for V0.27.3 per CIO 2026-05-10 finalized stack. P1 stack (3): drive_summary 12-field writer (B-059 / Spool Spec 3) + DriveDetector warm-restart misses short trips (I-019) + calibration.py types.py shadow + missing baselines table (I-018). P3 (1): drive_counter server-side sync gap (B-064). US-313 (B-062 drain_event close) DROPPED 2026-05-10 per CIO -- empirical Drain Test 11 + historical DB review show drains 10/11/12/13/14 all closed cleanly; Spool's 4-of-4 unclosed report did not match DB state. B-062 closes wontfix; US-307 forensic instrumentation already shipped in V0.27.2 stands-watch for any future occurrence.

EXECUTION ORDER (deps-aware): TIER 1 (P1, parallel) -- US-310 (drive_summary 12-field) + US-311 (DriveDetector warm-restart) + US-312 (calibration.py rename + baselines migration). TIER 2 (P3, parallel-with-Tier-1) -- US-314 (drive_counter sync gap).

THEME: 'V0.27.2 deploy + bench Drain 11 unmasked the next layer.' Bugs surfaced from Sprint 28 IRL operational test (Drives 8/9/10) + Spool's housekeeping clean-up of obd2db + bench drain forensics. Each story has a synthetic test that would FAIL pre-fix (per feedback_runtime_validation_required.md) AND a real-world validation gate captured in the sprint validation block.

CONSTRAINTS (anti-blocker discipline + Sprint 28 lessons): (a) US-274 file-existence check ran during grooming. (b) US-282 commit-vs-claim verifier active. (c) BL-010 + verify-table-names rule applied. (d) feedback_pm_verify_diagnostic_premises.md: PM reproduced calibration.py shadow empirically + REFUTED Spool's DriveDetector debounce hypothesis (MIN_INTER_DRIVE_SECONDS=5 not minutes); story scopes reflect verified premises. (e) feedback_pm_run_pre_flight_during_grooming.md: PM ran each story's pre-flight rg during grooming + baked findings into scope.filesToTouch (Sprint 28 had 50% pre-flight defect rate; this sprint targets 0). (f) CIO 2026-05-10 chain-end-merge: ALL implementation commits land on sprint/sprint29-bugfixes-V0.27.3; nothing merges to main until WHOLE V0.27 chain is fully functional working system.

GROUNDING SOURCES: Spool 2026-05-09 sprint-28-bug-fixes-consolidated note (Bug 2 P1 reclassification = B-059 Spec 3). Spool 2026-05-10 three-drives-tonight note (Bug A DriveDetector = I-019; Bug B drain close = B-062; drive_counter sync gap = B-064). CIO 2026-05-09 directive (calibration.py = I-018). Plus V0.27.2 BL-012 + BL-013 context (forensic instrumentation evidence + recorder seam pattern).

POST-SPRINT-29 ACTION ITEMS (NOT in sprint.json per CIO no-human-tasks rule): (1) CIO completes B-063 fuse-box wiring (still gated for in-vehicle drives). (2) Drive 11+ post-fuse-box first clean car-coupled drive. (3) Drain Test 12 post-V0.27.3 deploy validates B-062 fix held. (4) Once V0.27 chain fully clean, /chain-validated (or equivalent) merges all V0.27.X to main as new fully-functional-working stable.

stories.title: drive_summary 12-field writer contract enforcement (B-059)
stories.intent: Spool Spec 3 + housekeeping Item 2 P1: drives 3+4+5 currently have NULL on all analytics columns (start_time / end_time / duration_seconds / row_count / is_real / data_source). Pi-sync wrote keys; server-side analytics writer that populates fields 3-8 never ran. Drives 6-10 have NO rows because the broader US-304 writer regression blocked them entirely (now fixed in V0.27.2). V0.27.3 fixes the SERVER-SIDE analytics writer per Spool's 12-field contract: every drive_end + sync round-trip should produce a fully-populated drive_summary row within 30s. Without this, Spool's per-drive grading + analysis_history + drive_statistics are downstream-blocked.

Respect _PRESERVE_ON_UPDATE; race-handling per Spec 3 -- Pi-sync writes 1+2+9-12 first, analytics writes 3-8 second; UPSERT by (source_device, source_id) with NULL preservation)

feedback.grounding: Pre-flight audit (acceptance #1) confirmed _ensureDriveSummary at src/server/services/analysis.py:758 with caller at line 927 (enqueueAutoAnalysisForSync). Trigger path: post-sync -> extractDriveBoundaries unpacks connection_log drive_start/drive_end pairs -> per-drive _ensureDriveSummary -> _computeDriveAnalytics queries realtime_data filtered by drive_id (post-US-200 path). Spec 3 race-handling preserved: UPDATE branch writes ONLY fields 3-8 + device_id (Pi-sync 9-12 untouched); analytics-first INSERT leaves 9-12 NULL so later Pi-sync UPSERT lands on the same row. Pre-fix _ensureDriveSummary hardcoded is_real=True regardless of data quality and used connection_log timestamps for start_time/end_time -- both contradicted Spec 3. RED phase verified 7 contract assertions failed against pre-fix code (rowCount<100 -> True/expected None; zero-realtime stub -> START not None; mixed mostly-replay -> True/expected False; backfill ModuleNotFoundError x3); GREEN phase 12/12 + 58 across all affected files + 4169 fast-suite (was 4149 baseline). SCOPE-DRIFT NOTE: tests/server/test_drive_summary_reconciliation.py + tests/server/test_drive_report_single_row.py needed assertion updates because the new contract changes when is_real becomes TRUE (data-quality-derived, not hardcoded) and when start_time becomes non-NULL (realtime MIN, not connection_log boundary). PM scope.filesToTouch listed only the new test file; the two existing test updates are scope additions documented per BL-010 pattern. LEGACY-PATH DESIGN: driveId=None code path preserves connection_log boundaries as natural-key seed for back-compat with sync auto-analysis test + historical-replay idempotency; only data-quality fields (row_count / is_real / data_source) flow from realtime_data via time-range filter on the legacy path. REAL-WORLD VALIDATION GATE deferred to Drive 11+ (post-B-063 fuse-box wiring). Backfill --dry-run smoke not run against live chi-srv-01 obd2db (server access not available in this Pi-side dev session); CLI is import-clean + unit-tested in TestBackfillScript (3 tests). 4169 passed / 17 skipped / 0 failed in fast suite. Ruff clean across all 5 modified files. sprint_lint clean (0 err / 0 warn / 4 stories).

stories.title: DriveDetector warm-restart misses short trips (I-019)
stories.intent: I-019 Bug A: Spool 2026-05-10 evening test captured 1,078 NULL-drive_id rows in a 37-min orphan window between Drive 8 and Drive 9. A 2-3 min around-the-block trip in that window never fired drive_start / drive_end. Spool's 'debounce window' hypothesis was REFUTED by PM pre-flight (MIN_INTER_DRIVE_SECONDS=5; Mike waited 1+ min between drives; debounce wouldn't fire). Bug exists but root cause is elsewhere -- four candidate paths to investigate (PowerDownOrchestrator KEY_OFF state-reset / connection_log state tracking / RPM-poll pause-resume / ApplicationOrchestrator init blocker on warm-restart). V0.27.3 ships investigation + targeted fix + integration test.

src/pi/obdii/orchestrator/lifecycle.py (UPDATE only if root cause is in warm-restart init path; otherwise out of scope -- document drift in completionNotes)

groundingRefs.value: Empirical evidence (2026-05-10): Drive 8 clean (8268 rows), 37-min orphan window with 1078 NULL rows + ~3 min real driving in middle, Drive 9 captured. DriveDetector NEVER fired drive_start for the around-the-block test
Hypothesis-refuted-at-pre-flight: Spool said debounce window. Code shows MIN_INTER_DRIVE_SECONDS=5; 1+ min gap wouldn't trip. Real cause is elsewhere -- 4 candidate paths in I-019
Real-world validation gate: post-fix Test A/B/C protocol on Drive 12+ (post-fuse-box) MUST produce drive_id=N/N+1/N+2 (no orphan); requires B-063 hardware fix

acceptance: Pre-flight audit: read full DriveDetector state machine (detector.py); investigate the 4 candidate hypothesis paths; document which root cause matches in completionNotes
Targeted fix lands per identified root cause (single-file or multi-file as needed; phantom-path drift documented per BL-010 pattern if scope shifts)

Existing DriveDetector tests stay GREEN (no regression in cold-start / long-drive paths)

If 4-hypothesis investigation surfaces NONE of the candidate paths and bug is something else entirely -- STOP, document new hypothesis; PM revises scope

filesActuallyTouched: src/pi/obdii/orchestrator/core.py (UPDATE -- new _resetEngineOnEscalation method that clears _engineOnEscalated + _consecutiveAlternatorActiveSamples; docstring on _maybeEscalateOnAlternatorActiveSignature updated from 'one-shot per process' to 'one-shot per drive cycle' citing US-311 / I-019)

src/pi/obdii/orchestrator/event_router.py (UPDATE -- _handleDriveEnd now calls _resetEngineOnEscalation defensively via getattr so partial mocks survive; reset runs BEFORE the sync trigger + external onDriveEnd callback so any post-end state observer sees the re-armed flags)

tests/pi/integration/test_drive_detector_warm_restart_e2e.py (NEW -- 1 class / 1 test driving Test A/B/C protocol through real ApplicationOrchestrator + DriveDetector + tmp ObdDatabase; pre-fix discriminator confirmed in RED phase: assertion 'Test B precondition: _handleDriveEnd MUST clear _engineOnEscalated' FAILS -- pinpoints I-019 root cause exactly)

scripts/backfill_orphan_drive_id.py (NEW -- Pi obd.db backfill CLI; --start/--end/--target-drive-id; --rpm-filter narrows to RPM>500 windows so 30+ min of engine-off baseline is not absorbed into synthetic drive; --dry-run sentinel + --execute requires sentinel + .bak-us311 backup + single-transaction UPDATE; idempotent recompute-and-skip-on-equal pattern via NULL-only scan)

tests/pi/integration/test_backfill_orphan_drive_id.py (NEW -- 4 classes / 6 tests covering scanOrphansInWindow window-bound + tagged-drive exclusion + narrowToRpmWindows bracket math + applyBackfill idempotency + CLI dry-run sentinel + CLI execute-without-sentinel refusal)
feedback.grounding: Pre-flight 4-hypothesis investigation per acceptance #1: H1 PowerDownOrchestrator KEY_OFF state -- no clear reset bug at this layer (stage state driven by power-source transitions, not drive boundaries); H2 Connection_log state-tracking -- DriveDetector writes connection_log but does NOT consume it for boundaries (hypothesis incorrect); H3 RPM/SPEED polling pause-resume -- US-221 ECU silent mode SCALES the poll interval but does not stop polling; US-225 pausePolling fires only on US-216 IMMINENT power stage, not drive_end (no clear bug); H4 ApplicationOrchestrator init blocker on warm-restart -- TD-036/TD-047 territory + Sprint 25 connect blocker, but ALSO encompasses orchestrator state that doesn't reset on warm-restart. ROOT CAUSE PINNED: src/pi/obdii/orchestrator/core.py:368 _engineOnEscalated initialized False once at construction, set True on first escalation (Drive A), early-exits at line 1117 'if self._engineOnEscalated: return False' on every subsequent BATTERY_V sample. Spool's 2026-05-09 evening 3-drive drill: Drive 8 cold-start escalated cleanly -> drive_id=8; Drive 8 ended; around-the-block within 37-min orphan window saw BATTERY_V > 13.8V trace re-arrive but escalation already True -> RPM probe never fired -> DriveDetector blind to engine-on -> 1078 NULL-drive_id rows captured (BATTERY_V baseline + RPM-window driving) without drive_start firing; Drive 9 then minted normally because the orchestrator restarted (one-shot reset on process restart is the ONLY pre-fix re-arm path). FIX: add _resetEngineOnEscalation method on ApplicationOrchestrator + call from _handleDriveEnd in event_router.py BEFORE the sync trigger so post-end state observers see re-armed flags. Multi-file change documented per BL-010 pattern: scope.filesToTouch listed src/pi/obdii/drive/detector.py + src/pi/obdii/orchestrator/lifecycle.py; actual fix landed in core.py + event_router.py because the broken state lives in ApplicationOrchestrator (not DriveDetector or lifecycle init). PM stop condition matches: lifecycle.py was conditional on root cause being in warm-restart init path; my finding is in warm-restart STATE path (similar territory, different module). RED phase: pre-fix test failed with 'Test B precondition: _handleDriveEnd MUST clear _engineOnEscalated... assert True is False' -- exact discriminator. GREEN phase: post-fix all 7 new tests pass + 51 detector/lifecycle tests stay green + 100 orchestrator tests stay green + 4176 fast suite (was 4169 baseline; +7 new). Ruff clean across all 5 modified files. sprint_lint clean. REAL-WORLD VALIDATION GATE: post-B-063 fuse-box wiring -> Drive 12+ in-vehicle Test A/B/C drill (cold drive A 10-15 min -> 2-5 min wait -> short drive B 2-3 min -> 5-10 min wait -> cold drive C 10-15 min) MUST produce drive_id=N/N+1/N+2 with no orphan window; backfill script repairs the historical 2026-05-09T23:40 -> 2026-05-10T00:16Z orphan window on chi-eclipse-01 obd.db (deferred -- not run in this dev session because Pi access is not available; --dry-run smoke is unit-tested via test_backfill_orphan_drive_id.py). REUSABLE PATTERN: 'one-shot per process' state flags are a load-bearing anti-pattern when combined with warm-restart cycles -- the standing rule going forward: any orchestrator state that gates a per-drive event MUST reset on drive_end OR be re-derived from observable inputs each cycle (not latched). The fix-shape is symmetric to V0.24.1's PowerSource enum identity bug: in both cases, the bug stayed silent because the test envelope didn't exercise the second cycle. EXIT no tag (single-agent mode + 2 P1 stories remain unblocked: US-312 / US-314); ralph.sh starts next iteration.

stories.title: calibration.py types.py shadow + missing baselines table (I-018)
stories.intent: I-018: `python src/server/analytics/calibration.py --calibrate --apply` crashes at import time due to TWO stacked failures. (1) `src/server/analytics/types.py` shadows the Python stdlib `types` module -- when calibration.py imports statistics -> re -> enum -> stdlib types, Python finds the local types.py instead. Same anti-pattern class as I-014 (Sprint 12 obd -> obdii rename). (2) Even if fix #1 lands, the `baselines` table doesn't exist on chi-srv-01 -- `src/server/db/models.py:703-728` defines the Baseline model but no migration creates it. V0.27.3 ships both fixes coupled per CIO 2026-05-10 'keep it lean' directive: rename + 4 importer updates + v0008 migration + integration test that the calibration CLI runs to completion.

FakeRunner replaces SSH + MariaDB so suite stays hermetic. RED phase confirmed by ImportError on collection -- the v0008 module did not exist pre-fix)

feedback.grounding: Pre-flight audit (acceptance #1) ran rg `from src.server.analytics.types|from .types` and surfaced 9 importers: 4 src/server/analytics ({advanced,basic,helpers,__init__}.py per PM grounding) + 2 src/server/reports ({trend_report,drive_report}.py -- PM MISSED) + 3 tests/server ({test_reports,test_analytics_basic,test_analytics_advanced}.py -- PM MISSED). PM grounding also asserted calibration.py imports from analytics.types -- empirically false; calibration.py imports from src.server.db.models only. RENAME executed via filesystem mv; modification history on the renamed file annotates the I-018 + Sprint 12 US-187 precedent. RED phase verified pre-fix discriminators: (a) test_calibrationModule_importsCleanly_asScript subprocess produced the EXACT I-018 ImportError 'cannot import name GenericAlias from types' with the local types.py named in Python's hint; (b) test_analyticsPackage_doesNotExposeShadowingTypesModule failed because src/server/analytics/types.py existed; (c) test_migration_0008_baselines.py failed at COLLECTION with ImportError 'cannot import name v0008_us312_create_baselines' (module did not exist). GREEN phase: 29 new tests + 200 affected analytics/reports/migration tests all pass; full fast suite 4205 passed / 17 skipped / 0 failed (was 4176 baseline post-US-311; +29 new); ruff clean across all 13 modified files (auto-fixed 6 F541 extraneous-f-string instances + alphabetized imports in 5 files). sprint_lint will warn on the 'src/server/analytics/types.py path does not exist on disk' check until PM regrooming reads the BL-010 scope-drift annotation in scope.filesToTouch (the entry now reads `src/server/analytics/analytics_types.py (RENAMED -- formerly...)` so the annotation lands in the same field). REAL-WORLD VALIDATION GATE deferred to post-V0.27.3 deploy: chi-srv-01 deploy-server.sh runs apply_server_migrations --run-all -> v0008 lands the baselines table -> `python scripts/report.py --calibrate --apply --device chi-eclipse-01` runs to completion + writes rows for drives 5+6+7. Backfill is not needed because the analytics-driven calibration path computes proposals from current realtime_data + statistics each invocation; once the table exists the workflow is reproducible from scratch. SCOPE-DRIFT NOTE per BL-010 pattern: 8 files outside PM's scope.filesToTouch were edited (5 importer rename + 1 migration registry + 2 test files). All 8 are documented in feedback.filesActuallyTouched + sprint.scope.filesToTouch with `BL-010 scope addition` annotation. PM pre-flight rg surfaces all 9 importers; standing recommendation for V0.27.4+ grooming: when scope.acceptance lists `rg X src/`, PM should run that exact rg AND tests/, since renames + cross-cutting refactors typically have test importers too. REUSABLE PATTERN: stdlib-shadow rename anti-pattern recurs (I-014 obd->obdii Sprint 12; I-018 types->analytics_types Sprint 29). Future detection: any subpackage `types.py` / `enum.py` / `re.py` / `os.py` etc. should fail a pre-commit lint check; standing recommendation filed at offices/pm/inbox if not already there.

stories.title: drive_counter server-side sync gap (B-064)
stories.intent: B-064: Pi has minted drive_id up to 10; server `obd2db.drive_counter.last_drive_id=3` (last good sync was Drive 3 era). Mirror writer for drive_counter table either isn't running or isn't covered by sync. Likely same Sweep-5 wiring drift family as US-304 + US-306. Not safety-critical -- realtime_data + connection_log + drive_summary all sync correctly -- but stale signal anyone querying drive_counter would trip over.

filesToTouch: src/pi/data/sync_log.py (UPDATE -- Pi-side cursor table writer; ensure drive_counter is in sync coverage list + delta-push covered. Pre-flight identifies whether actual fix lives here OR in src/pi/sync/client.py; phantom-path drift documented per BL-010 if scope shifts)

tests/pi/sync/test_drive_counter_sync.py (NEW -- integration test asserts Pi-side drive_counter advance triggers server-side update within 60s of next sync; would FAIL pre-fix because table is not in sync coverage)
filesToRead: src/pi/obdii/drive_id.py + drive_summary.py (Pi mints drive_id + writes drive_counter)
doNotTouch: drive_id minting logic on Pi (working correctly)
Server-side endpoints other than sync handler

groundingRefs.value: Empirical evidence (Spool 2026-05-10): SELECT last_drive_id FROM drive_counter on chi-srv-01 returns 3; Pi has minted up to 10 (next = 11). Last good sync was Drive 3 era (2026-04-23)
Likely same Sweep-5 wiring drift family as US-304 + US-306 (drive_id propagation lost in mixin refactor)
Real-world validation gate: post-V0.27.3 deploy + next IRL drive sync round-trip -> server drive_counter.last_drive_id matches Pi's most recent drive within 60s

acceptance: Pre-flight audit: rg `drive_counter` src/pi/sync/ + src/server/api/ -- map current sync coverage; identify why mirror isn't firing
drive_counter table covered by Pi sync delta push; server-side update propagates within 60s of sync completion
tests/pi/sync/test_drive_counter_sync.py: synthetic Pi mints drive_id=N -> sync triggers -> server drive_counter shows N within 60s; would FAIL pre-fix
invariants: Real-world validation gate: next IRL drive sync round-trip advances server drive_counter.last_drive_id to current Pi value
Existing realtime_data + connection_log + drive_summary sync paths unchanged
stopConditions: If sync coverage list location is NOT in src/pi/sync/ as expected -- STOP, document phantom-path drift (BL-010 pattern); ship to actual location
If the fix requires server-side schema change OR new sync endpoint -- STOP, file as architectural-ambiguity blocker

src/server/api/sync.py (UPDATE -- new DriveCounterData Pydantic model with gt=0 validation + new optional driveCounter field on SyncRequest + new runDriveCounterUpsert helper using GREATEST/MAX for monotonic upsert across MariaDB and SQLite + postSync wires the counter into the SAME transaction as runSyncUpsert so partial failures roll BOTH back; __all__ extended; BL-010 scope addition because PM scope.filesToTouch did not list this server file)

src/pi/sync/client.py (UPDATE -- new pushDriveCounter method + _readLocalDriveCounter helper + _postDriveCounterWithRetry method mirroring the existing per-table retry classifier; forcePush now appends a drive_counter PushResult after the per-table sweep so cadence-driven manual flushes carry the counter; BL-010 scope addition because PM scope.filesToTouch listed src/pi/data/sync_log.py + tests/pi/sync/test_drive_counter_sync.py but the actual Pi-side wire is in src/pi/sync/client.py -- sync_log.py is bookkeeping for delta tables and drive_counter is a singleton, not a delta table, so it does not fit the IN_SCOPE_TABLES contract)

tests/pi/sync/test_drive_counter_sync.py (NEW -- 7 tests / 2 classes; TestDriveCounterBundledInForcePush asserts at least one POST body carries driveCounter (RED pre-fix because forcePush iterated only IN_SCOPE_TABLES) + defensive cases for missing-table + counter-only-no-data sync; TestPushDriveCounterMethod direct-API surface tests OK + EMPTY + DISABLED + FAILED status semantics)

tests/server/test_drive_counter_sync.py (NEW -- 13 tests / 4 classes; TestDriveCounterModelContract pins ORM shape; TestDriveCounterRequestField validates the Pydantic surface including gt=0 rejection of zero/negative; TestRunDriveCounterUpsert covers fresh-insert + advance + GREATEST guard against rewind + idempotency + singleton invariant; TestDriveCounterIsNotInTableRegistry documents intentional absence from ACCEPTED_TABLES)

tests/pi/sync/test_sync_force_push.py (UPDATE -- BL-010 scope addition; TestSummaryAggregation.test_summaryCountsMatchPerTableStatuses bumped from len(IN_SCOPE_TABLES) to len(IN_SCOPE_TABLES)+1 since forcePush now appends the drive_counter PushResult)
tests/server/test_db_models.py (UPDATE -- BL-010 scope addition; TestTableCount.test_totalTableCount bumped 19 -> 20 with new docstring entry citing US-314 drive_counter)
feedback.grounding: Pre-flight audit (acceptance #1) ran rg `drive_counter` across src/pi/sync/ + src/server/api/ + src/server/db/. Mapped the sync architecture: DELTA_SYNC_TABLES (append-only monotonic INTEGER PK -- realtime_data, statistics, drive_summary, etc.) vs SNAPSHOT_TABLES (TEXT-keyed upsert -- profiles, vehicle_info; currently SKIPPED entirely with explicit 'future upsert-sync story' TODO from US-194). drive_counter is NEITHER -- it's a single-row INTEGER state mirror (CHECK id=1, holds last_drive_id). PM grounding 'Likely same Sweep-5 wiring drift family as US-304 + US-306' was MISLEADING -- drive_counter was NEVER in IN_SCOPE_TABLES on Pi or _TABLE_REGISTRY on server, and there was no DriveCounter ORM model on the server. The US-200 catch-up migration created the raw DDL table but no sync writer ever advanced it. Server's last_drive_id=3 is the seed value (or a manual ad-hoc bump) -- not evidence of broken historical sync. ROOT CAUSE: drive_counter sync was never wired (not a regression). FIX SHAPE: top-level `driveCounter: {lastDriveId: N}` field on the existing /api/v1/sync POST + new pushDriveCounter() method on Pi SyncClient + new runDriveCounterUpsert() helper on server with GREATEST/MAX guard against rewinds. The forward-only invariant matters because analytics joins on drive_id corrupt silently if the counter rewinds (e.g., a stale sync arriving after a fresher one). Test file pre-fix discriminator confirmed in RED phase: pytest collection failed with ImportError 'cannot import name DriveCounterData from src.server.api.sync' before any of the 13 server-side tests ran. Pi-side test fixture seeds drive_counter.last_drive_id=10 + invokes forcePush + asserts at least one POST body has driveCounter -- pre-fix forcePush iterated only IN_SCOPE_TABLES so no body carried the field. GREEN phase: 20 new tests + 2 existing-test scope additions all pass; full fast suite 4225 passed / 17 skipped / 0 failed (was 4205 baseline post-US-312; +20 new). Ruff clean across all 7 modified files. sprint_lint 0 err / 1 informational warn (US-312 size-vs-filesToTouch ratio carried over from prior story; not US-314). DESIGN CHOICE: bundled top-level field rather than building out the post-US-194 SNAPSHOT_TABLES upsert path because (a) drive_counter is a singleton, not a TEXT-keyed snapshot table; (b) extending SNAPSHOT_TABLES would expand scope well beyond a P3 hygiene story; (c) the dedicated field keeps the natural-key contract on (source_device, source_id) untouched for tables that actually use it. KNOWN LIMITATION: the server drive_counter table is single-row across devices (no source_device column). Multi-device deployment would have devices overwrite each other; today only chi-eclipse-01 is in production so this is acceptable. Adding source_device would require a schema migration and is out of scope for B-064 P3. REAL-WORLD VALIDATION GATE: post-V0.27.3 deploy + next IRL drive sync round-trip -> server `SELECT last_drive_id FROM drive_counter` advances to current Pi value (10+) within 60s of sync completion. Backfill is automatic -- the next forcePush from the Pi pushes the singleton to 10. SCOPE-DRIFT NOTE per BL-010 pattern: PM scope.filesToTouch listed src/pi/data/sync_log.py (UPDATE) + tests/pi/sync/test_drive_counter_sync.py (NEW), 2 files. Actual scope was 7 files: 3 src/ (Pi client + server sync + server models) + 4 tests/ (2 new + 2 existing test count bumps). The PM phantom-path note in the original scope (`phantom-path drift documented per BL-010 if scope shifts`) covers this. PM grounding's 'src/pi/data/sync_log.py' guess was wrong -- sync_log.py is the bookkeeping table for delta-by-PK syncs, and drive_counter is a singleton that doesn't fit the IN_SCOPE_TABLES contract; the actual wire-up belongs in src/pi/sync/client.py. Standing recommendation for V0.27.4+ grooming: when the bug touches the sync data path, PM scope should include both src/pi/sync/client.py AND src/pi/data/sync_log.py + the server's _TABLE_REGISTRY in src/server/api/sync.py + ORM models -- delta-table additions touch sync_log.py; singleton/upsert additions touch client.py + models.py. EXIT: Sprint 29 P1+P3 stack now COMPLETE 4 of 4 (US-310 + US-311 + US-312 + US-314); ralph.sh emits <promise>COMPLETE</promise>.
